definition: A SKOS concept can be viewed as an idea or notion; a unit of thought. However, what constitutes a unit of thought is subjective, and this definition is meant to be suggestive, rather than restrictive.
notes: Disjoint with ConceptScheme and Collection.

definition: A SKOS concept scheme can be viewed as an aggregation of one or more SKOS concepts. Semantic relationships (links) between those concepts may also be viewed as part of a concept scheme.
notes: Disjoint with Concept and Collection. A thesaurus, classification scheme, subject heading list, or taxonomy is an example of a concept scheme.

definition: A meaningful collection of concepts.
notes: Disjoint with Concept and ConceptScheme. Useful for labeling a group of concepts that are not defined as a broader concept.

definition: An ordered collection of concepts, where order is significant.
notes: Sub-class of Collection. Use memberList (not member) to describe the ordered sequence.

definition: The preferred lexical label for a resource, in a given language.
notes: No two concepts in the same ConceptScheme may have the same prefLabel in the same language tag. A resource can have at most one prefLabel per language.

definition: An alternative lexical label for a resource.
notes: Covers acronyms, abbreviations, spelling variants, and irregular plural/singular forms. A string may be both altLabel and hiddenLabel.

definition: A lexical label for a resource that should be hidden when displaying the resource to the user, but should still be accessible to free text search operations.
notes: Used for misspellings or non-canonical forms that should be findable but not shown in the UI.

definition: A notation is a string of characters such as '300' or 'T58.5' used to uniquely identify a concept within the scope of a given concept scheme.
notes: Unlike lexical labels, notations are not meant to be human-readable natural language strings. A concept may have multiple notations.

definition: A general note, for any purpose.
notes: Super-property of all documentation properties: definition, scopeNote, example, historyNote, editorialNote, changeNote.

definition: A statement or formal explanation of the meaning of a concept.
notes: Sub-property of note.

definition: A note that helps to clarify the meaning and/or the use of a concept.
notes: Sub-property of note.

definition: An example of the use of a concept.
notes: Sub-property of note.

definition: A note about the past state/use/meaning of a concept.
notes: Sub-property of note.

definition: A note for an editor, translator or maintainer of the vocabulary.
notes: Sub-property of note. Not intended for display to end-users.

definition: A note about a modification to a concept.
notes: Sub-property of note.

definition: Relates a resource to a concept scheme in which it is included.
notes: A concept may be a member of more than one concept scheme.

definition: Relates a concept scheme to a concept which is topmost in the broader/narrower concept hierarchies for that scheme.
notes: Inverse of topConceptOf. Used on the ConceptScheme resource.

definition: Relates a concept to the concept scheme that it is a top level concept of.
notes: Inverse of hasTopConcept. Used on the Concept resource.

definition: Links a concept to a concept that is related in some way.
notes: Super-property of broader, narrower, and related.

definition: Relates a concept to a concept that is more general in meaning.
notes: By convention not used to link concepts in different concept schemes. Sub-property of semanticRelation.

definition: Relates a concept to a concept that is more specific in meaning.
notes: Inverse of broader. Sub-property of semanticRelation.

definition: skos:broaderTransitive is a transitive super-property of skos:broader.
notes: Not intended for direct use; its instances are inferred from the broader hierarchy.

definition: skos:narrowerTransitive is a transitive super-property of skos:narrower.
notes: Not intended for direct use; inferred from the narrower hierarchy.

definition: Relates a concept to a concept with which there is an associative semantic relationship.
notes: Symmetric. Must not be used to link a concept to itself or to a concept that is already linked via broader/narrower.

definition: Relates two concepts coming from different concept schemes.
notes: Super-property of exactMatch, closeMatch, broadMatch, narrowMatch, relatedMatch. Cross-vocabulary alignment only.

definition: Indicates a high degree of confidence that two concepts can be used interchangeably across a wide range of information retrieval applications.
notes: Transitive and symmetric. Sub-property of mappingRelation and closeMatch.

definition: Indicates that two concepts are sufficiently similar that they can be used interchangeably in some information retrieval applications.
notes: Symmetric but not transitive. Less strong than exactMatch.

definition: Relates a concept to a concept in an external scheme that has a more general meaning.
notes: Inverse of narrowMatch. Sub-property of mappingRelation.

definition: Relates a concept to a concept in an external scheme that has a more specific meaning.
notes: Inverse of broadMatch. Sub-property of mappingRelation.

definition: Relates a concept to a concept in an external scheme where there is an associative semantic relationship.
notes: Symmetric. Sub-property of mappingRelation.

definition: Relates a collection to one of its members which is either a concept or another collection.
notes: Domain: Collection.

definition: Relates an ordered collection to the RDF list containing its members.
notes: Domain: OrderedCollection. Use instead of member for ordered sequences.
